ops.title: Yasyf requires all registry publishes (npm, PyPI, GitHub releases) to go through tag/dispatch-triggered CI, never local credentials
ops.body:
When a release needs publishing to any registry, use the repo's CI publish path; if none exists, create one modeled on the sibling repos, then dispatch it. Never `npm publish`/`twine`/`flit` locally.

**Why:** Yasyf said "use the CI publish path dont publish locally, mirror the publishing in cc-interact in cc-present CI if its not there" after a local npm publish attempt hit a dead token. Local tokens rot; OIDC trusted publishing doesn't.

**How to apply:** House patterns — npm: tag/dispatch workflow with `id-token: write`, `registry-url`, `npm publish --access public` behind an already-published version guard, and the package.json must carry a `repository.url` (provenance validation rejects without it); the workflow must run the package's full build script (cc-interact's first publish shipped without `.d.ts` by running bare `vite build`). PyPI: tag-triggered trusted publishing that must run in the repo's own workflow, not a reusable one (OIDC `job_workflow_ref`). Go binaries: goreleaser via `yasyf/homebrew-tap` reusable workflows. Registering an npm trusted publisher is a one-time npmjs.com UI step only Yasyf can do (Cloudflare blocks automation). Related: [[parallel-sessions-own-the-working-copy]].
